title: Fluffy Canadian Resinous Burrito
description: A monsoon-inspired dish featuring shredded Mexican cheese, cure, and plant-based goodness. Perfect for a cozy Canadian evening.

ingredients.name: Cauliflower rice
ingredients.notes: Food processor or mandoline for optimal texture.

ingredients.name: Shredded Mexican cheese alternative
ingredients.notes: Vegan-friendly, made from tapioca starch and coconut oil.

ingredients.name: Diced onions
ingredients.notes: Chop into small pieces for even distribution.

ingredients.name: Diced bell peppers
ingredients.notes: Assorted colors for a pop of color.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: To taste, adjust according to preference.

ingredients.name: Vegan sour cream
ingredients.notes: For a tangy and creamy element.

ingredients.name: Salsa
ingredients.notes: Homemade or store-bought, adjust to taste.

instructions: In a large bowl, mix together the cauliflower rice, chia seed cure, shredded Mexican cheese alternative, canned black beans, diced onions, minced garlic, olive oil, diced bell peppers, chopped cilantro, lime juice, salt, and black pepper until well combined.
Transfer the mixture to a baking dish and cover with foil. Bake for 25 minutes, then remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 10-15 minutes until lightly browned.
While the mixture is baking, warm the tortilla wraps in the oven or a dry pan.
Assemble the burritos by spooning the baked mixture onto a tortilla, topping with vegan sour cream, and serving with a side of salsa.
Garnish with additional cilantro, if desired.
